input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Why:** Provides iOS SDK, simulators, and build toolchain. Required even for MAUI iOS builds.
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch    # installs additional components
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Version discovery:** Check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the minimum supported Xcode version for your target .NET/MAUI release.

---

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — download the latest **LTS** or **STS** SDK for macOS (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Why:** Includes the `dotnet` CLI, runtime, and base SDK. MAUI workloads install on top of this.
- **Version discovery:**
  ```bash
  dotnet --list-sdks          # shows installed SDKs
  dotnet --version             # shows active SDK
  ```
  The [MAUI support policy page](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) lists which .NET SDK versions are supported.

---

## 3. MAUI Workload

- **Install via CLI (recommended):**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload install maui
  ```
  This pulls in all sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `android`, `ios`, etc.
- **Version discovery:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list          # shows installed workloads
  dotnet workload search maui   # shows available workloads
  ```

---

## 4. Android SDK & Java JDK

The `maui-android` workload installs the **Android SDK** and a compatible **JDK (Microsoft OpenJDK)** automatically. You typically don't need to install these separately.

- **If you need manual control:** Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager.
- **Environment variables** (set in `~/.zshrc` if needed):
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"   # or the workload-managed path
  export JAVA_HOME="/usr/local/share/dotnet/packs/Microsoft.Android.Sdk.Darwin/..." 
  ```
- **Version discovery:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list                              # confirms android workload
  ~/.android/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list  # if using Android Studio
  ```

---

## 5. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) | Free, lightweight, .NET MAUI extension available |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | Discontinued — **do not install** | Reached end-of-life August 2024 |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) | Full MAUI support, paid license |

For VS Code, also install the **.NET MAUI extension**:
```bash
code --install-extension ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui
```

---

## 6. Verification

```bash
# 1. Confirm toolchain
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list            # should show "maui" (or sub-workloads)
xcodebuild -version             # Xcode version + build tools

# 2. Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios       # adjust TFM to your .NET version

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# 3. Run on simulators/emulators
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four build/run commands succeed, your environment is fully functional.

---

## Recommended install order

1. **Xcode** (longest download — start first)
2. **.NET SDK**
3. `dotnet workload install maui`
4. **VS Code + extensions** (or Rider)
5. **Verify** with a test project

All sources above are official Microsoft or Apple channels. The single most important reference to bookmark is the [official MAUI getting-started guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation), which tracks version requirements as they change.
